description:
Cleanup ticket — file now, do after 22.12 lands.

The halt_kind plumbing landed in fc1ff766 (halt_kind in closure flags high 2 bits, threaded through fz_alloc_closure / init_static_closures / fz_aot_register_static_closure / fz_spawn_entry) was load-bearing for the original 22.6 force-drop attempt because uniform main bodies needed to pick the matching halt-cont singleton at runtime.

With 22.11's per-spec direct dispatch, every fn's halt-cont can be selected at compile time from its chain_repr at the entry spec. The runtime header bits and the FFI arity-3 fz_alloc_closure may now be pure overhead.

## Scope

1. Audit every reader of closure header halt_kind bits:
   - fz_spawn_entry shim's halt-cont selection (runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs region)
   - fz_main_entry path
   - fz_resume_park
   - AOT shim (runtime/src/aot_shim.rs line 282-285 hardcoded Tagged)

2. For each, determine whether the halt-cont can instead be selected at compile time and baked into the per-fn entry shim. If yes:
   - Revert fz_alloc_closure to arity 2.
   - Reclaim the high 2 flags bits.
   - Simplify init_static_closures / fz_aot_register_static_closure FFI to drop halt_kind.
   - Delete fn_halt_kinds map (src/ir_codegen.rs:2735+).

3. If the runtime needs halt_kind for genuinely dynamic reasons (e.g., remote spawn from an unknown caller), document and keep.

## Acceptance

- cargo test --workspace green.
- runtime/src/heap.rs and fz_value.rs simpler; closure header flags has fewer used bits.
- AOT shim no longer hardcodes Tagged — it derives halt_kind from the AOT'd module's main fn spec.

## BLOCKED BY

- fz-ul4.27.22.12 [[fz-cps-seam.force-drop]]
title: fz-cps-seam.retire-halt-kind-plumbing: reassess halt_kind closure-header plumbing post-22.7
